title: Subscription Economics & Funnel Brief — Padel AI Coach
scope: JD requirements: unit economics, funnels, retention, LTV/CAC, subscription product experience.
calculation_method: python_explicit (arithmetic in pricing_scenarios is reproducible by reviewer)

A_pricing_anchors.verified_quote: Strava Premium $11.99 per month or $79.99 per year
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Anchor for B2C sport-tech freemium; closest social-graph + segmentation analogue.

A_pricing_anchors.verified_quote: WHOOP One $199 / Peak $239 / Life $359 per year
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Annual-only billing maximises commitment; padel pre-pay annual could replicate this anchor.
A_pricing_anchors.reverify_risk: WHOOP 5.0 launched in Q2 2025 introduced new tier names (Performance / Peak / Life). If the One tier disappears from the public membership surface, this pricing row needs immediate re-verification against the screenshot baseline.

A_pricing_anchors.verified_quote: SwingVision Pro $14.99 per month or $179.99 per year
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Closest racket-sport CV analogue; sets ceiling for padel premium pricing in EUR/USD.

A_pricing_anchors.verified_quote: Your Games at $250 per team per season
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: B2B per-team-per-season anchor; informs hedge-model coach co-pilot pricing if B2C WTP fails.

A_pricing_anchors.verification_method: Inherited from evidence/10_monetization.json captured during 20260501T135005Z run; pricing page exists at clutchapp.io/pricing but the EUR 53/month figure is a Sonar paraphrase, not a vendor-page screenshot.
A_pricing_anchors.data_gap: Live page does not surface tier price; figure is from prior Sonar paraphrase only — tagged INFERRED rather than VERIFIED.
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Direct padel competitor; club-side hardware-anchored pricing — non-comparable to B2C app.

A_pricing_anchors.verified_quote: Software & Service Plan costs €70/month per court
A_pricing_anchors.verification_method: WebSearch 2026-05-03 — aggregator captured price; vendor product page lists 'Prices on request'
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Hardware-locked pricing; sets per-court B2B floor independent of player B2C pricing.

A_pricing_anchors.verification_method: Inherited from evidence/10_monetization.json prior run; live page no longer surfaces standard rate card. The EUR 107/month figure is a Sonar paraphrase, not a vendor-page screenshot.
A_pricing_anchors.data_gap: Live page does not republish per-court rate; figure is from prior Sonar paraphrase only — tagged INFERRED rather than VERIFIED.
A_pricing_anchors.rationale_for_padel: Booking-side incumbent; B2B floor for any tournament/club integration revenue line.
Strongest B2B incumbent reference for tiered SaaS bundling; 7.4x spread Standard→Champion suggests upsell elasticity.

stages.name: Awareness (impression to landing-page visit)
stages.data_gap: No padel-specific awareness conversion data published; CPM proxies only.
stages.comment: Use Foundry CRO 2026 CPM inflation 20% YoY as cost proxy; conversion rate is channel-specific.

stages.name: Landing page → app install / signup
stages.verified_quote: median landing page conversion rate is 6.6% across industries

stages.name: Install → activation event (first match captured)
stages.verified_quote: onboarding conversion install to activation 30-50% considered healthy
stages.comment: Activation defined here as first match video captured + first insight delivered.
stages.padel_specific_friction: Phone-camera capture protocol (mount, angle, height) is the dominant drop-off risk per peer cards (Padelytics, Eyes On Padel).

stages.name: First match captured → first insight delivered
stages.comment: No published benchmark; padel CV pipelines (Joao-M-Silva/padel_analytics) confirm technical feasibility but conversion-loss not measured.
stages.instrumentation_target: Track latency capture→insight; aim ≤24h to keep momentum.
stages.data_gap: Padel-specific install→insight conversion is unpublished; first-party benchmark required.

stages.name: Free user → first paid month (within 30 days of activation)
stages.kill_threshold: Below 4% triggers hedge model per evidence/10_monetization.json wtp_test.

stages.name: Freemium app — overall download to paid subscriber
stages.comment: Confirms Foundry CRO 5-9% figure refers to activated users, not raw downloads.

stages.name: First paid month → 90-day retention (paid)
stages.derivation_explicit: monthly churn ≤7% → 90-day survival = (1 − 0.07)^3 ≈ 80% upper-bound; 50-60% is the conservative lower-band reflecting padel-specific seasonality risk (FM-103). The 35-45% Foundry CRO benchmark is a 12-month figure, not a 90-day figure; the 50-60% band is INFERRED from the underlying churn rate, not a Foundry-published benchmark.
stages.comment: Lower-band band reflects padel-specific seasonality risk (FM-103).
stages.data_gap: Padel-specific 90-day paid retention is unpublished; nearest analogue is Strava social-fitness retention claims. Tag changed from VERIFIED_INHERITED to INFERRED to match red-team feedback.

monthly_churn_inputs.b2c_paid_months_1_to_3_source: Sport-tech B2C benchmark per Strava and Whoop public communications, captured in evidence/10_monetization.json
monthly_churn_inputs.strava_retention_quote: Strava bucks broader app churn via social-fitness flywheel

fx_rates_used.fx_rate_source: ECB indicative rate range Q1-Q2 2026; sensitivity not material at single decimal of LTV ratio.

scenarios.label: Lean — Portugal anchor EUR 6.49/month
scenarios.monthly_churn_source: Mid-range of 6-9% sport-tech B2C; assumption_strength: anchored

scenarios.label: Anchor — Spain/Italy/EUR EUR 7.99/month
scenarios.monthly_churn_source: Mid-range with mild retention boost from primary-geo language fit; assumption_strength: inferred

scenarios.label: Premium — UAE AED 49/month
scenarios.arpu_source: evidence/10_monetization.json geo_price_localization AE row; FX AED→EUR ECB indicative
scenarios.monthly_churn_source: Premium-tier audiences typically churn lower; assumption_strength: inferred

blended_cac_eur.paid_meta_uae_uplift_calc: USD 94 base * 1.4 UAE premium / 1.085 = 121.29 (Python-recomputed from 121.66 to fix arithmetic per red-team MEDIUM-1)
blended_cac_eur.paid_meta_uae_uplift_source: INFERRED — UAE digital media premium per evidence/12_geo.json AE assumptions

scenarios_appendix_ru_direct_channel.label: RU Direct-channel — RUB 499/month (PPP-indexed EUR 5.0)
scenarios_appendix_ru_direct_channel.monthly_churn_source: Upper-band of sport-tech B2C 6-9% range; assumption_strength: inferred — no Strava-class flywheel data exists for Russian-speaking padel.

blended_cac_eur.organic_telegram_newsletter_calc: USD 12 / 1.085 = 11.06 — Foundry CRO 2026 baseline carried forward as proxy; no Russia-specific CAC benchmark available
blended_cac_eur.paid_meta_data_gap: Meta is unavailable in the Russian advertising channel mix at this date.
blended_cac_eur.apple_search_ads_data_gap: Apple Search Ads Russian payment rails are constrained at this date.

scenarios_appendix_ru_direct_channel.channel_constraint: Telegram-only; per evidence/20260501T135005Z/11_gtm.json#CH-006 the channel reduces to Telegram newsletter plus organic Telegram-bot distribution.
data_gaps: Russia-specific CAC benchmark; Foundry CRO USD 12 figure is a proxy.
Meta and Apple Search Ads unavailable in this market — paid CAC math is structurally null.
scenarios_appendix_ru_direct_channel.h_04_implication: RU LTV:CAC organic = 5.32 clears the 3:1 SaaS guardrail at honest 8.5% churn. H-04 in 04_30_60_90_plan.md remains as a smoke-test hypothesis (not a kill gate) because the Russian channel-mix is structurally constrained — paid Meta and ASA are unavailable, so a kill threshold below 4% paid conversion would force the channel to fall back to organic-only, which is the only mode the unit economics validate.

interpretation.headline: Organic newsletter / community-led acquisition is the only channel that clears the 3:1 LTV:CAC guardrail in every scenario. Paid Meta is a net-positive only above the EUR 7.99 anchor and at churn ≤7%. Affiliate coach acquisition is below 1:1 across all scenarios — coach affiliate must be retained as a B2B2C funnel, not a B2C CAC line.
interpretation.implication: Channel mix discipline matters more than absolute pricing. The published Foundry CRO USD 12 newsletter CAC is the channel that bends the LTV:CAC math; everything else is a hedge.

D_retention_levers.name: Pair-anchored progress (rating-with-partner)
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Rating updates only after both partners' uploads; the partner becomes a retention asset, not a feature.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Strava social-fitness flywheel converts engagement into retention
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: Demonstrates technical feasibility of pair-anchored events

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of paid users with a paired partner active in last 30 days
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <40% of paid users have an active paired partner at month 3 → retention will collapse

D_retention_levers.name: Drill prescription tied to last match
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Each match recap closes with one drill prescription. The drill is the bridge between insight and next session.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Whoop personalises strain and recovery guidance per member
peer_proof.primary_analogue_source_caveat: third-party paraphrase of Whoop coaching surface — actual quote is from an Alibaba-hosted wellness affiliate page, not the Whoop vendor page. The whoop.com/us/en/membership/ surface is the vendor reference but does not contain this verbatim phrasing.
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: Padelytics positions match-level breakdowns; prescription layer is the unbuilt extension

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of recaps that lead to a drill check-off within 14 days
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <15% drill engagement → recap is a vanity surface, not a behaviour driver

D_retention_levers.name: Streak / weekly cadence
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Weekly tactical recap newsletter + in-app weekly digest with a visible streak counter that resets on missed weeks.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Learners with 7-day streaks 2.4x more likely to return next day
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: Strava 14B kudos in 2025 evidences social streak compounding

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of paid users on a 4+ week streak
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <20% sustained streak share → cadence is decorative, not addictive

D_retention_levers.name: Pre-match habit trigger
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Push notification 30 minutes before a booked court (deep-linked to Playtomic/MATCHi calendar) prompting capture setup checklist.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Headspace anchors meditation habit to calendar trigger windows
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: Playtomic exposes booking metadata; trigger surface is reachable via deep link

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of booked sessions that result in a capture
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <25% capture-on-booked-session → the trigger is wrong or capture friction is too high
D_retention_levers.data_gap: Headspace habit-trigger literature cited generally; specific Headspace retention numbers not in this run.

D_retention_levers.name: Coach handoff for student dashboard
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Player can route weekly recap pack to a coach. Coach uses it at renewal conversation. Coach becomes a retention bias for the player.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Hudl Assist subscriptions sold per team per season
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: CoachLogic LMS for coaches; documented switching cost

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of player uploads opting into coach handoff
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <5% handoff rate → coach co-pilot path becomes coach-only per evidence/11_gtm.json PLG-005

D_retention_levers.name: Public rating asset (SEO-indexable profile)
D_retention_levers.mechanism: Each paid user gets an indexable public rating page; loss of subscription means loss of public-facing rating credibility.

peer_proof.primary_analogue_quote: Strava public profile compounds athlete identity over time
peer_proof.padel_evidence_role: Padelboard demonstrates leaderboard appetite in Nordic markets

D_retention_levers.instrumentation_metric: % of paid users with public profile views ≥10/month
D_retention_levers.kill_threshold: <10% organic profile traffic → identity flywheel is not engaging

E_subscription_playbook.lesson: Anchor commitment with annual prepay; resist rolling-monthly billing
E_subscription_playbook.source_quote: WHOOP does not offer rolling monthly subscription pay annually
E_subscription_playbook.mechanism: Annual prepay forces user to skip the cancel-after-month-1 default; LTV anchors at full year.
E_subscription_playbook.padel_application: Offer EUR 79/year vs EUR 7.99/month. Surface the annual saving (17% discount) at first paywall.

E_subscription_playbook.lesson: Make the daily streak visible at every entry point
E_subscription_playbook.source_quote: Streaks reinforce a daily lesson loop with immediate reward
E_subscription_playbook.mechanism: Visible streak compounds psychological cost of breaking a habit.
E_subscription_playbook.padel_application: Weekly streak (not daily — padel cadence is weekly). Reset visible 24h before expiry; recovery via single uploaded match.

E_subscription_playbook.lesson: Convert the social graph into the retention engine
E_subscription_playbook.source_quote: Strava 2.23% interaction rate 14B kudos given in 2025
E_subscription_playbook.mechanism: Friends provide free retention signal; cancellation cost rises with social ties.
E_subscription_playbook.padel_application: Pair-anchored progress (RL-001) — partner becomes a retention asset; both lose progress on cancel.

E_subscription_playbook.lesson: Tie subscription to a measurable outcome the user controls
E_subscription_playbook.source_quote: SwingVision Pro $14.99 per month or $179.99 per year
E_subscription_playbook.mechanism: Outcomes (line calls, scores) are tangible and paid value is unambiguous.
E_subscription_playbook.padel_application: Rating delta per month is the headline KPI surfaced in the recap; subscription is the mechanism that produces the delta.

E_subscription_playbook.lesson: Use coach as an unpaid distribution channel that increases switching cost
E_subscription_playbook.source_quote: Hudl Assist subscriptions sold per team per season
E_subscription_playbook.mechanism: Coach owns the workflow; player follows the coach; switching the player out forces coach buy-in.
E_subscription_playbook.padel_application: PLG-005 coach handoff per evidence/11_gtm.json. Coach reputation graph (community_surfaces) is the moat.

F_failure_modes.name: Capture friction kills activation
F_failure_modes.description: Phone-camera capture protocol (mount, angle, height, lighting) drops install→activation below the 30% sport-tech floor.
F_failure_modes.kill_experiment: Ship two onboarding variants: (A) phone-mount tutorial video + checklist; (B) club-camera fallback via Eyes On Padel partnership. A/B 200 installs in Spain over 4 weeks. Variant winning ≥35% activation continues.
F_failure_modes.verification_method: INFERRED from peer-card friction discussion in evidence/04_peer_cards/

F_failure_modes.name: Rating becomes commodity (federation publishes free)
F_failure_modes.description: If FIP/national federations publish a free derived rating, the paid rating loses its credibility moat. Premier Padel adjacency makes this credible within 18 months.
F_failure_modes.diagnostic_signal: Federation announces derived rating OR Playtomic ships derived levels; paid conversion drops 30%+ in 8 weeks
F_failure_modes.kill_experiment: Run a structured survey of 200 Playtomic / MATCHi power users in Spain + Italy: 'If your booking app published a derived rating, would respondents still pay EUR 7.99 for the alternative product?' Threshold: ≥30% retain WTP.

F_failure_modes.name: Churn after season-end (Q4 collapse)
F_failure_modes.description: Padel is outdoor-skewed in Iberia; winter players migrate or pause. October-March monthly churn could spike 2-3x baseline.
F_failure_modes.diagnostic_signal: Month-over-month paid churn jumps from 7% (base) to ≥14% in October
F_failure_modes.kill_experiment: Instrument Q4 churn cohort by geography. If Iberia spikes, ship indoor-court partnership content + winter rating-prep drill series. Re-measure at 8 weeks.
F_failure_modes.verification_method: INFERRED — seasonality risk per FIP regional data; not isolated in current evidence run.
F_failure_modes.data_gap: Padel-specific seasonal churn elasticity is unpublished; this is the most urgent first-party measurement.

data_gaps.reason: No published padel-specific benchmark for capture→insight conversion rate.

Tag demoted from VERIFIED_INHERITED to INFERRED per red-team MEDIUM-2; the 50-60% 90-day band is derived from monthly churn (1-0.07)^3, not directly published. Padel-specific 90-day paid retention remains unpublished.

Live clutchapp.io/pricing did not surface monthly figure in 2026-05 verification; tag demoted from VERIFIED_INTERNAL_CAPTURE to INFERRED per red-team HIGH-4 — figure is a Sonar paraphrase, not a vendor-page screenshot.

Live matchi.com no longer publishes per-court Business tier rate card; tag demoted from VERIFIED_INTERNAL_CAPTURE to INFERRED per red-team HIGH-4 — figure is a Sonar paraphrase, not a vendor-page screenshot.
Padel-specific Q4 paid-churn data is unpublished. Most urgent first-party measurement before scaling Iberia paid acquisition.
FX rates EUR/USD/AED inferred from ECB indicative ranges; not material at one-decimal LTV ratio precision but should be re-validated at financial close.

Russia-specific CAC benchmark is unpublished; the USD 12 organic newsletter figure is carried forward from Foundry CRO 2026 as a proxy. Meta and Apple Search Ads are structurally unavailable in the Russian channel-mix at this date — paid CAC math is null, not low.

WHOOP 5.0 launched in Q2 2025 introduced new tier names. The One/Peak/Life set captured 2026-05-03 may be re-released or re-tiered; weekly re-verification SLA documented. Screenshot artefact at screenshots/whoop.png is the captured baseline.
